Term Sheet: Proposed acquisition by Kencana Capital Sdn Bhd (Reg. No. KCS-2001137) of 60% of Bunga Raya Tech Berhad (Reg. No. BRT-2010453), a company listed on the Main Board of Bursa Putra Exchange.
The parties acknowledge Malaysian securities law obligations; no unpublished price-sensitive information remains after Cleansing Note CN-2026-05 issued by Bunga Raya Tech Berhad on 31 May 2026.
Consideration: RM118,000,000 cash funded via a RM80,000,000 term loan from Tanjung Bayu Bank Berhad (commitment letter obtained) and RM38,000,000 equity from Kencana Capital Sdn Bhd; no new securities will be issued by the Target prior to completion.
Conditions precedent: shareholder approvals, Bursa Putra Exchange waiver (if any), and clearance from the Malaysian Markets Conduct Commission; long-stop date 30 September 2026.
Data protection: Schedule D (Data Processing Addendum) and Compliance Memo CM-2026-07 form part of this term sheet and incorporate PDPA requirements, including retention limits and data minimisation commitments for HR and customer records.
Cross-border transfer: any transfer of personal data to OrkidCloud International Ltd’s data centre in the Republic of Meranti requires Data Export Approval DEA-006; the prior overseas transfer arrangement was decommissioned on 30 April 2026 and no live cross-border flows are active pre-signing.
DSAR and erasure: all data subject access requests received up to 31 May 2026 have been fulfilled and closed; the last erasure request (ER-2026-014) was completed on 15 May 2026 under Retention Schedule RS-2025-01.
Consent and minimisation: processing for employee onboarding is based on contract and legal obligation; the consent withdrawal workflow (POL-CW-02) remains available but there are no pending withdrawals; only strictly necessary fields (role, payroll ID, contact) will be shared in diligence.
Onboarding forms: OB-29 (Employment Onboarding Form) and PN-PDPA-03 (Employee PDPA Notice) will be reissued post-completion; this term sheet does not by itself trigger any collection, use, or cross-border transfer of personal data.
Data sharing perimeter: pre-completion data rooms will contain anonymised, aggregated, or synthetic datasets only; the legacy data broker engagement with Kelapa Data Brokers Sdn Bhd terminated on 1 March 2026.
Bursa-style disclosure: a summary announcement will be made to the Bursa Putra Exchange upon signing of the definitive SPA; no financial forecasts or undisclosed valuation sensitivities are included herein, and the parties will maintain an insider list policy-only until cleansing.
Covenants: the Target shall maintain its PDPA retention schedule and avoid any material change to HR systems; encryption keys remain hosted in-country and will not be exported or shared with third-country processors prior to completion.
Conditions subsequent: Kencana Capital to submit an updated DPA Schedule and obtain internal Data Export Approval DEA-006 within 15 business days post-completion; failure to obtain approval does not invalidate completion but defers any cross-border transfers.
Contact and governing law: Ms. Nur Aina Salleh (Company Secretary) at corpsec@bungarayatech.example and Encik Farid Hakim (Kencana Capital) at deals@kencanacapital.example; this term sheet is governed by the laws of Malaysia with disputes heard in the courts of Kuala Lumbar.
